content:
I started to implement a Rust module for the kernel crate called clist. The idea is that it will allow to perform list operations on lists that were allocated and created on the C side but now need to be processed on the rust side. I need this for DRM buddy allocator rust bindings. The C side allocates a list of blocks and creates a C-side circular doubly linked list out of them. On the rust side, we have to iterate over the list and call C side bindings on the list_head of different blocks. The clist module will also provide bindings to common C list helpers like list_del and list_empty.

Just wanted to share the idea here as I work on it. Looking forward to any initial comments on the idea here.

Cc: @Alice Ryhl

I'm pretty sure rust/kernel/list.rs can be leveraged for this, i.e. no new implementation required.

I'm (also) still a bit confused how list.rs is supposed to be used and when a KVec should be preferred.

I don't see any direct examples for using List with C, but I suppose the policy for all those core data structures (see especially the Red Black tree) is that they shall be C-compatible. I don't see that officially stated somewhere, though.

Maybe Alice can clarify and maybe we can give some guidance in the docu.
The List is not necessarily C compatible.
Do you see the rust List as usable for the purpose? AFAIK, the rust List is for Rust-owned / Rust-allocated lists.
The Rust list is designed to avoid the list_head in the List struct itself.

right, also it uses "list links" which I think is incompatible with lists allocated on the C side. If there is any meaning/reason in modifying list.rs, let me know. Otherwise I'll continue along my clist idea

I'm pretty sure rust/kernel/list.rs can be leveraged for this, i.e. no new implementation required.

I already looked into and considered it. But looking forward to hearing if anyone thinks that is still feasible.
My main concern is whether a single abstraction is a good idea for all c lists. C lists are part of a larger C abstraction with various ownership rules and so on.
Exactly. The clist idea is about working with lists coming from the C side with rules decided by C (list_head, RCU protection needs, locking needs, etc), not managing its own..
Feel free to +Cc me once there is some code / RFC. I'm interested in that

Philipp Stanner said:

I'm (also) still a bit confused how list.rs is supposed to be used and when a KVec should be preferred.

Just like in C it depends on the specific use-case. KVec is just a resizable array, so when this is what you need, use KVec.
Yeah and kvec is an array not a linked list which is what this thread is about.
